core_mechanism: crossed-product construction of an operator algebra — adjoining a group-action (time-translation) generator to an algebra carrying a modular/KMS equilibrium state — in which the tracial (KMS) equilibrium property breaks down and the algebra factorizes into a free product (asymptotic free independence of subalgebras) once higher-order out-of-time-order correlations decay to zero
brief_reason: The abstract forms a crossed-product algebra by quantizing the observer's clock, shows the equilibrium state fails to be a trace (breakdown of the KMS condition), and finds that as the out-of-time-ordered correlators decay past the scrambling time a free product algebra emerges.
